data.title: Ansys reports 10% Q4 growth in what might be last before acquisition by Synopsys
data.description:
Ansys (NASDAQ:ANSS) reported 10% year-over-year revenue growth in its fourth quarter 2024 financial results, which might be the last the company reports before it is acquired by Synopsys (NASDAQ:SNPS).

Ansys topped consensus estimates with the results. Shares remained relatively static during early post-market trading on Wednesday, following the quarterly report.

For the quarter ended December 31, Anysys reported adjusted earnings per share of $4.44 which was more than the consensus estimate of $3.86. Revenue for the quarter totaled $882.2M, which was also more than the estimate of $864.46M.

Ansys, which develops engineering simulation software, is currently in the process of being acquired by Synopsys (NASDAQ:SNPS) for $35B. The companies expect the deal to close during the first half of 2025 following all regulatory approvals.

"The U.K. Competition and Markets Authority provisionally accepted our remedies towards a transaction approval in Phase 1," Ansys said in its release. "The State Administration for Market Regulation of the People's Republic of China has officially accepted our filing, and its review of the proposed transaction is in process. We continue to work with the regulators in other relevant jurisdictions to conclude their reviews. The transaction is anticipated to close in the first half of 2025, subject to the receipt of required regulatory approvals and other customary closing conditions.

The deal won European Union approval last month. Synopsys agreed to sell its Optical Solutions business to Keysight Technologies (KEYS) and announced the sale of the PowerArtist unit, owned by Ansys, to Keysight.

Due to the pending acquisition, Ansys is no longer conducting earnings calls or providing guidance.

data.title: Brookdale projects 4.75%-5.75% RevPAR growth and $430M-$445M EBITDA for 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Brookdale Senior Living Inc. (NYSE:BKD) Q4 2024

Management View

  • Brookdale reported Q4 RevPAR at the top of its guidance and adjusted EBITDA exceeding expectations. CEO Lucinda Baier highlighted a 140 basis point increase in weighted average occupancy for 2024 and a 6.1% annual RevPAR growth. Adjusted EBITDA improved by over 15%, while adjusted free cash flow increased nearly 40%.
  • Management noted disruptions in lead flow from third-party referral partners, which impacted move-ins starting in Q2. To mitigate this, the company reallocated marketing efforts to internal channels and strengthened relationships with local referral sources. Fourth-quarter move-ins were 8% above pre-pandemic levels and marked the strongest Q4 move-ins since 2016.
  • Strategic transactions in 2024 included lease restructurings and acquisitions. Baier emphasized that these portfolio changes would enhance cash flow, reduce escalating lease costs, and increase owned real estate to over 75% of consolidated units.
  • CFO Dawn Kussow reported that fourth-quarter residency revenue grew 3.9% year-over-year, with consolidated RevPAR increasing 5.5%. Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter reached approximately $99 million, surpassing the top of the guidance range.

Outlook

  • For 2025, management forecasts RevPAR growth of 4.75% to 5.75% and adjusted EBITDA between $430 million and $445 million. These projections incorporate assumptions about occupancy growth and rate trends, as well as the anticipated disposition of 55 Ventas communities by October 1, 2025.
  • Baier highlighted efforts to expand the Brookdale HealthPlus program to 60 additional communities in 2025 and implement the Brookdale EngagementPlus initiative to enhance resident experiences.
  • Management anticipates continued steady occupancy growth supported by favorable supply-demand dynamics and the aging population.

Financial Results

  • Adjusted EBITDA increased 15% year-over-year to $99 million in Q4, benefiting from revenue growth, effective cost management, and favorable lease terms. Same-community operating income grew 4.4%, driven by RevPAR and labor expense improvements.
  • Adjusted free cash flow for Q4 was negative $12 million, impacted by seasonal real estate tax payments, but positive adjusted free cash flow was achieved in the second half of 2024.
  • Notable expenses included $3.5 million in natural disaster costs related to Hurricanes Helene and Milton. General and administrative expenses were 4.7% of revenue, a 30 basis point improvement from the prior year.

Q&A

  • Tao Qiu, Macquarie: Asked about the RevPAR impact of the Ventas lease amendment and occupancy/rate assumptions. CFO Kussow explained that the 4.75%-5.75% RevPAR guidance includes the anticipated disposition of 55 communities and reflects move-in volumes and improved attrition rates.
  • Josh Raskin, Nephron Research: Inquired about Brookdale's long-term EBITDA growth rate and the timeline to achieve pre-pandemic occupancy levels. CEO Baier emphasized disciplined profitable growth rather than targeting a specific occupancy rate.
  • Ben Hendrix, RBC: Asked about the low-acuity move-in trend and its relation to referral source changes. Baier noted that lower-acuity residents typically result in longer stays, which supports occupancy growth over time.
  • Joanna Gajuk, Bank of America: Questioned Q1 occupancy expectations amid a challenging flu season. Kussow reported strong January occupancy trends and noted that December-to-January sequential declines were better than the prior two years.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised concerns about the impact of third-party referral disruptions and the sustainability of occupancy growth. Their tone was neutral to slightly positive, focusing on strategic initiatives and long-term growth potential.
  • Management remained optimistic, highlighting significant progress in RevPAR, EBITDA growth, and strategic portfolio changes. Baier's remarks reflected confidence in operational improvements and market dynamics.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management appeared more assured in addressing challenges related to referral sources and portfolio management.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted to emphasize steady and sustainable growth, reflecting improved confidence in addressing third-party referral disruptions.
  • Strategic focus evolved with expanded initiatives like HealthPlus and EngagementPlus, reflecting broader efforts to enhance differentiation.
  • Analysts' tone remained consistent, with a focus on occupancy trends and long-term growth strategies. However, there was increased scrutiny on expense management and natural disaster impacts.
  • Adjusted EBITDA grew sequentially from $92.2 million in Q3 to $99 million in Q4, supported by revenue growth and cost efficiencies.

Risks and Concerns

  • Persistent disruptions in third-party referral sources could continue to impact move-ins.
  • Seasonal challenges, including flu outbreaks and natural disasters, may affect occupancy trends and operational costs.
  • Analysts highlighted the need for effective execution of strategic initiatives to sustain growth amid competitive pressures.
  • Interest rate variability remains a potential headwind for free cash flow.

Final Takeaway

Brookdale Senior Living's Q4 2024 results underscored significant progress in occupancy and financial performance, supported by strategic portfolio actions and operational improvements. Key initiatives like HealthPlus and EngagementPlus aim to strengthen differentiation and drive long-term growth. While challenges in third-party referral sources and seasonal risks persist, management's guidance for 2025 reflects confidence in sustainable growth and profitability.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Liberty Latin America reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Liberty Latin America press release (NASDAQ:LILAK): Q4 Revenue of $1.15B (-0.9% Y/Y) beats by $10M.
  • ~100,000 organic fixed and mobile subscriber net adds in 2024
  • Robust revenue & Adj. OIBDA growth in C&W Caribbean, Panama & Costa Rica
  • Puerto Rico remains challenged; rebuilding in 2025
  • ~400,000 homes passed or upgraded using FTTH; 97% of footprint Gigabit-ready
  • Successfully refinanced $3.3bn of C&W credit silo debt in last 6 months.

data.title: Blue Owl Capital reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Blue Owl Capital press release (NYSE:OBDC): Q4
  • Fourth quarter net investment income ("NII") per share of $0.47, in-line with the prior quarter and exceeding the regular dividend by 27%
  • Based on OBDC's supplemental dividend framework, the Board of Directors (the "Board") declared a fourth quarter supplemental dividend of $0.05 per share
  • Total dividends for the fourth quarter were $0.42 per share, representing an annualized dividend yield of 11.0% based on fourth quarter net asset value ("NAV") per share
  • NAV per share of $15.26, as compared with $15.28 as of September 30, 2024, driven by unrealized losses from credit marks in the portfolio, partially offset by over-earning of the base dividend
  • New investment commitments for the fourth quarter totaled $1.7 billion, offset by $1.6 billion of sales and repayments, as compared with $1.2 billion of new investment commitments and $1.1 billion of sales and repayments for the three months ended September 30, 2024. The weighted average total yield of debt and income producing investments was 11.1% at fair value
  • Investments on non-accrual decreased to 0.4% of the portfolio at fair value, as compared with 0.6% as of September 30, 2024
  • Shares -0.45%.

data.title: IMAX misses on profit and revenue in Q4; shares fall
data.description:
Premium movie theater chain IMAX (NYSE:IMAX) on Wednesday missed on core metrics in its fourth quarter earnings report, causing its shares to dive 5% after the bell on the NYSE.

At the end of 2024, the company delivered 146 system installations, at the high end of the initial installation guidance of 120 to 150.

Top grossing titles in Q4 included Gladiator 2 ($31M), Venom: The Last Dance ($31M), Mufasa: The Lion King ($26M) and a rerelease of the movie Interstellar ($20M).

"With the headwinds we faced in China in 2024 beginning to lift, we’re poised to build on the Fourth Quarter’s significant double-digit growth in adjusted EBITDA and earnings per share. We look forward to capitalizing on the promising opportunity ahead to achieve a record year at the global box office and strong returns for our shareholders," IMAX CEO Rich Gelfond said in a statement.

For 2025, the company expects to deliver "a record box office" of more than $1.2B, a total adjusted EBITDA margin of 40%+, and system installations of 145 to 160.

For the three months ended December 31, the company's net income was $5.3M or $0.10 per share, compared with $2.5M or $0.05 per share a year earlier.

Excluding items, the Mississauga, Canada-based company earned 27 cents, missing the average analyst estimate by 4 cents.

Revenue rose nearly 8% to $92.7M and missed the consensus estimates.

data.title: First Quantum Minerals announces pricing and upsizing of senior notes offering
data.description:
• First Quantum Minerals (TSX:FM:CA) said on Wednesday that it has successfully completed the pricing of its offering of $1B aggregate principal amount of 8.000% senior notes due 2033.
  • The original offering amount of the Notes of $750M has been increased to $1B. The issue price of the Notes is 100.000%.
  • Interest on the Notes will accrue from the issue date at 8.000% per annum and will be payable semi-annually. Settlement is expected to take place on or about March 5, 2025. The Notes will be senior unsecured obligations of the company and will be guaranteed by certain of the company's subsidiaries.
  • The company intends to apply the gross proceeds from the sale of the Notes, together with cash on balance sheet, to repay a $250M portion of its revolving credit facility, to fund the partial tender offer for its existing 6.875% senior notes due 2027 and to pay transaction fees, costs and expenses.

data.title: GRI Bio announces 1-for-17 reverse stock split to regain Nasdaq compliance
data.description:
• GRI Bio (NASDAQ:GRI) board approved a 1-for-17 reverse stock split of the company’s common stock.
  • The reverse split will legally take effect at 4:01 p.m. Eastern Time, on February 21, 2025.
  • The company’s common stock will open for trading on Nasdaq on February 24, 2025, on a split-adjusted basis.
  • The reverse split is intended to increase the per-share trading price of GRI’s common stock to enable the company to regain compliance with the minimum bid price requirement for continued listing on Nasdaq.
  • The reverse split will reduce the number of shares of outstanding common stock from ~8.93M shares to ~525,492 shares.
  • GRI shares fall nearly 23% post-market on Wednesday.
  • Source: Press release
